name: stem

metadata.name: stem
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 (LGPLv3)
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules
metadata.summary: Stem is a Python controller library that allows applications to interact with Tor (https://www.torproject.org/).

documentation:
For tutorials and API documentation see [Stem's homepage](https://stem.torproject.org/).

# Quick Start

To install you can either use...

```
pip install stem
```

... or install from the source tarball. Stem supports both the python 2.x and 3.x series. To use its python3 counterpart you simply need to install using that version of python.

```
python3 setup.py install
```

After that, give some [tutorials](https://stem.torproject.org/tutorials.html) a try! For questions or to discuss project ideas we're available on [irc](https://www.torproject.org/about/contact.html.en#irc) and the [tor-dev@ email list](https://lists.torproject.org/cgi-bin/mailman/listinfo/tor-dev).

modules.name: test.arguments
documentation: Commandline argument parsing for our test runner.
Copyright 2015-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.arguments.parse
documentation:
Parses our arguments, providing a named tuple with their values.

:param list argv: input arguments to be parsed

:returns: a **named tuple** with our parsed arguments

:raises: **ValueError** if we got an invalid argument

functions.name: test.arguments.get_help
documentation:
Provides usage information, as provided by the '--help' argument. This
includes a listing of the valid integration targets.

:returns: **str** with our usage information

functions.name: test.arguments.crop_module_name
documentation:
Test modules have a 'test.unit.' or 'test.integ.' prefix which can
be omitted from our '--test' argument. Cropping this so we can do
normalized comparisons.

:param str name: module name to crop

:returns: **str** with the cropped module name

modules.name: test.output
documentation:
Variety of filters for the python unit testing output, which can be chained
together for improved readability.
Copyright 2011-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.output.thread_stacktraces
documentation:
Provides a dump of the stacktrace information for all active threads.

:returns: **dict** that maps thread names to their stacktrace

functions.name: test.output.apply_filters
documentation:
Gets the tests results, possibly processed through a series of filters. The
filters are applied in order, each getting the output of the previous.

A filter's input arguments should be the line's (type, content) and the
output is either a string with the new content or None if the line should be
omitted.

:param str testing_output: output from the unit testing
:param list filters: functors to be applied to each line of the results

:returns: str with the processed test results

functions.name: test.output.colorize
documentation: Applies escape sequences so each line is colored according to its type.

functions.name: test.output.strip_module
documentation:
Removes the module name from testing output. This information tends to be
repetitive, and redundant with the headers.

functions.name: test.output.runtimes
documentation: Provides test runtimes if showing verbose results.

functions.name: test.output.align_results
documentation:
Strips the normal test results, and adds a right aligned variant instead with
a bold attribute.

variables.name: test.output.SUPPRESS_STDOUT
documentation: prevent anything from being printed to stdout

classes.name: test.output.ErrorTracker

methods.name: test.output.ErrorTracker.register_error
documentation:
If called then has_errors_occured() will report that an error has occured,
even if we haven't encountered an error message in the tests.

methods.name: test.output.ErrorTracker.set_category
documentation:
Optional label that will be presented with testing failures until another
category is specified. If set to None then no category labels are included.

For tests with a lot of output this is intended to help narrow the haystack
in which the user needs to look for failures. In practice this is mostly
used to specify the integ target we're running under.

:param str category: category to label errors as being under
Stores any failure or error results we've encountered.

modules.name: test.unit.util.system
documentation:
Unit tests for the stem.util.system functions. This works by mocking the
stem.util.system.call function to selectively exercise other functions. None of
these tests actually make system calls, use proc, or otherwise deal with the
system running the tests.

functions.name: test.unit.util.system.mock_call
documentation:
Provides mocking for the system module's call function. There are a couple
ways of using this...

- Simple usage is for base_cmd is the system call we want to respond to and
  responses is a list containing the respnose. For instance...

  mock_call('ls my_dir', ['file1', 'file2', 'file3'])

- The base_cmd can be a formatted string and responses are a dictionary of
  completions for tat string to the responses. For instance...

  mock_call('ls %s', {'dir1': ['file1', 'file2'], 'dir2': ['file3', 'file4']})

:param str base_cmd: command to match against
:param list,dict responses: either list with the response, or mapping of
  base_cmd formatted string completions to responses

:returns: **functor** to override stem.util.system.call with

modules.name: test.unit.connection.authentication
documentation:
Unit tests for the stem.connection.authenticate function.

Under the covers the authentiate function really just translates a
PROTOCOLINFO response into authenticate_* calls, then does prioritization
on the exceptions if they all fail.

This monkey patches the various functions authenticate relies on to exercise
various error conditions, and make sure that the right exception is raised.

documentation:
Does basic validation that all valid use cases for the PROTOCOLINFO input
and dependent functions result in either success or a AuthenticationFailed
subclass being raised.

modules.name: test
documentation:
Unit and integration tests for the stem library. Helpers include...

```
get_new_capabilities - missing capabilities found while testing
register_new_capability - note that tor feature stem lacks

get_all_combinations - provides all combinations of attributes
tor_version - provides the version of tor we're testing against
```
Copyright 2011-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.get_new_capabilities
documentation:
Provides a list of capabilities tor supports but stem doesn't, as discovered
while running our tests.

:returns: **set** of (type, message) tuples for the capabilities

functions.name: test.register_new_capability
documentation:
Register new capability found during the tests.

:param str capability_type: type of capability this is
:param str msg: description of what we found
:param str suppression_token: skip registration if this token's already been
  provided

functions.name: test.get_all_combinations
documentation:
Provides an iterator for all combinations of a set of attributes. For
instance...

```
>>> list(test.get_all_combinations(['a', 'b', 'c']))
[('a',), ('b',), ('c',), ('a', 'b'), ('a', 'c'), ('b', 'c'), ('a', 'b', 'c')]
```

```
param list attr

attributes to provide combinations for

param bool include_empty

includes an entry with zero items if True

returns

iterator for all combinations
```

functions.name: test.tor_version
documentation:
Provides the version of tor we're testing against.

```
param str tor_path

location of tor executable to cehck the version of

returns

 of tor invoked by our integration
tests
```

Check the 'hidserv-rend-relayed-cells' and 'hidserv-dir-onions-seen', which

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.extrainfo_descriptor.TestExtraInfoDescriptor.test_padding_counts

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.extrainfo_descriptor.TestExtraInfoDescriptor.test_locale_mapping_lines

Uses valid and invalid data to tests lines of the form...
"<keyword>" CC=N,CC=N,...

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.extrainfo_descriptor.TestExtraInfoDescriptor.test_minimal_bridge_descriptor
documentation: Basic sanity check that we can parse a descriptor with minimal attributes.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.extrainfo_descriptor.TestExtraInfoDescriptor.test_bridge_ip_versions_line
documentation: Parses the 'bridge-ip-versions' line, which only appears in bridges.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.extrainfo_descriptor.TestExtraInfoDescriptor.test_bridge_ip_transports_line

modules.name: test.integ.interpreter
documentation: Tests invocation of our interpreter.

modules.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo
documentation:
Integration tests for the stem.response.protocolinfo.ProtocolInfoResponse class
and related functions.

methods.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo.TestProtocolInfo.test_parsing
documentation:
Makes a PROTOCOLINFO query and processes the response for our control
connection.

methods.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo.TestProtocolInfo.test_get_protocolinfo_path_expansion
documentation:
If we're running with the 'RELATIVE' target then test_parsing() will
exercise cookie path expansion when we're able to query the pid by our
prcess name. This test selectively disables system.call() so we exercise
the expansion via our control port or socket file.

This test is largely redundant with test_parsing() if we aren't running
with the 'RELATIVE' target.

methods.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo.TestProtocolInfo.test_multiple_protocolinfo_calls
documentation:
Tests making repeated PROTOCOLINFO queries. This use case is interesting
because tor will shut down the socket and stem should transparently
re-establish it.

methods.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo.TestProtocolInfo.test_pre_disconnected_query
documentation:
Tests making a PROTOCOLINFO query when previous use of the socket had
already disconnected it.

methods.name: test.integ.response.protocolinfo.TestProtocolInfo.assert_matches_test_config
documentation:
Makes assertions that the protocolinfo response's attributes match those of
the test configuration.

modules.name: test.integ.client
documentation: Integration tests for stem.client.

modules.name: test.integ.client.connection
documentation: Integration tests for establishing a connection with tor's ORPort.

methods.name: test.integ.client.connection.TestConnection.test_invalid_arguments
documentation: Provide invalid arguments to Relay.connect().

methods.name: test.integ.client.connection.TestConnection.test_not_orport
documentation: Attempt to connect to an ORPort that doesn't exist.

methods.name: test.integ.client.connection.TestConnection.test_no_common_link_protocol
documentation: Connection without a commonly accepted link protocol version.

methods.name: test.integ.client.connection.TestConnection.test_connection_time
documentation: Checks duration we've been connected.

methods.name: test.integ.client.connection.TestConnection.test_established
documentation: Successfully establish ORPort connection.

modules.name: test.integ.installation
documentation: Tests installation of our library.

functions.name: test.integ.installation._assert_has_all_files
documentation:
Check that all the files in the stem directory are present in the
installation. This is a very common gotcha since our setup.py
requires us to remember to add new modules and non-source files.

:raises: **AssertionError** files don't match our content

methods.name: test.integ.installation.TestInstallation.test_install
documentation:
Installs with 'python setup.py install' and checks we can use what we
install.

methods.name: test.integ.installation.TestInstallation.test_sdist
documentation:
Creates a source distribution tarball with 'python setup.py sdist' and
checks that it matches the content of our git repository. This primarily is
meant to test that our MANIFEST.in is up to date.

modules.name: test.integ
documentation: Integration tests for the stem library.

modules.name: test.integ.directory
documentation: Integration tests for stem.directory.

modules.name: test.integ.directory.authority
documentation: Integration tests for stem.directory.Authority.

methods.name: test.integ.directory.authority.TestAuthority.test_cache_is_up_to_date
documentation: Check if the cached authorities we bundle are up to date.

modules.name: test.integ.manual
documentation:
Integ testing for the stem.manual module, fetching the latest man page from the
tor git repository and checking for new additions.

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_escapes_non_ascii
documentation:
Check that our manual parser escapes all non-ascii characters. If this
fails then that means someone probably added a new type of non-ascii
character. Easy to fix: please simply add an escape for it in
stem/manual.py's _get_categories().

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_parsing_with_indented_lines
documentation:
Our ExitPolicy's description is an interesting one for our parser in that
it has indented lines within it. Ensure we parse this correctly.

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_that_cache_is_up_to_date
documentation: Check if the cached manual information bundled with Stem is up to date or not.

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_attributes
documentation:
General assertions against a few manual fields. If you update tor's manual
then go ahead and simply update these assertions.

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_has_all_categories
documentation:
Check that the categories in tor's manual matches what we expect. If these
change then we likely want to add/remove attributes from Stem's Manual
class to match.

methods.name: test.integ.manual.TestManual.test_has_all_tor_config_options
documentation: Check that all the configuration options tor supports are in the man page.

modules.name: test.integ.directory.fallback
documentation: Integration tests for stem.directory.Fallback.

methods.name: test.integ.directory.fallback.TestFallback.test_cache_is_up_to_date
documentation: Check if the cached fallbacks we bundle are up to date.

methods.name: test.integ.directory.fallback.TestFallback.test_fallback_directory_reachability
documentation:
Fetch information from each fallback directory to confirm that it's
available.

modules.name: test.integ.util
documentation: Integration tests for stem.util.* contents.

modules.name: test.integ.process
documentation: Tests the stem.process functions with various use cases.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_version_argument
documentation: Check that 'tor --version' matches 'GETINFO version'.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_help_argument
documentation: Check that 'tor --help' provides the expected output.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_quiet_argument
documentation: Check that we don't provide anything on stdout when running 'tor --quiet'.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_hush_argument
documentation: Check that we only get warnings and errors when running 'tor --hush'.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_hash_password
documentation:
Hash a controller password. It's salted so can't assert that we get a
particular value. Also, tor's output is unnecessarily verbose so including
hush to cut it down.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_hash_password_requires_argument
documentation:
Check that 'tor --hash-password' balks if not provided with something to
hash.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_dump_config_argument
documentation: Exercises our 'tor --dump-config' arugments.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_validate_config_argument
documentation: Exercises our 'tor --validate-config' argument.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_list_fingerprint_argument
documentation: Exercise our 'tor --list-fingerprint' argument.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_list_torrc_options_argument
documentation: Exercise our 'tor --list-torrc-options' argument.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_no_orphaned_process
documentation:
Check that when an exception arises in the middle of spawning tor that we
don't leave a lingering process.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_torrc_arguments
documentation: Pass configuration options on the commandline.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_torrc_arguments_via_stdin
documentation: Pass configuration options via stdin.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_with_missing_torrc
documentation: Provide a torrc path that doesn't exist.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_unanonymous_hidden_service_config_must_match
documentation:
Checking that startup fails if HiddenServiceNonAnonymousMode and
HiddenServiceSingleHopMode don't match.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_can_run_multithreaded
documentation:
Our launch_tor() function uses signal to support its timeout argument.
This only works in the main thread so ensure we give a useful message when
it isn't.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_launch_tor_with_config_via_file
documentation: Exercises launch_tor_with_config when we write a torrc to disk.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_launch_tor_with_config_via_stdin
documentation: Exercises launch_tor_with_config when we provide our torrc via stdin.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_with_invalid_config
documentation: Spawn a tor process with a configuration that should make it dead on arrival.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_launch_tor_with_timeout
documentation: Runs launch_tor where it times out before completing.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_take_ownership_via_pid
documentation:
Checks that the tor process quits after we do if we set take_ownership. To
test this we spawn a process and trick tor into thinking that it is us.

methods.name: test.integ.process.TestProcess.test_take_ownership_via_controller
documentation:
Checks that the tor process quits after the controller that owns it
connects, then disconnects..

methods.name: test.integ.util.conf.TestConf.test_load_multiline

methods.name: test.integ.util.conf.TestConf.test_save

methods.name: test.integ.util.proc.TestProc.test_stats

Checks for our control port in the stem.util.proc.connections output if
we have one.

Integration tests for stem.util.connection functions against the tor process

modules.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_metrics_consensus
documentation: Checks if consensus documents from Metrics are parsed properly.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_real_consensus
documentation:
Checks that version 3 consensus documents from chutney can be properly
parsed.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_metrics_vote
documentation: Checks if vote documents from Metrics are parsed properly.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_vote
documentation: Checks that vote documents are properly parsed.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_minimal_consensus
documentation: Parses a minimal network status document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_minimal_vote
documentation: Parses a minimal network status document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_examples
documentation: Run something similar to the examples in the header pydocs.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_signature_validation
documentation: Check that we can validate the consensus with its certificates.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_handlers
documentation:
Try parsing a document with DocumentHandler.DOCUMENT and
DocumentHandler.BARE_DOCUMENT.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_parse_file
documentation: Try parsing a document via the _parse_file() function.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_missing_fields
documentation: Excludes mandatory fields from both a vote and consensus document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_unrecognized_line
documentation: Includes unrecognized content in the document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_duplicate_fields
documentation:
Almost all fields can only appear once. Checking that duplicates cause
validation errors.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_version
documentation:
Parses the network-status-version field, including trying to handle a
different document version with the v3 parser.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_vote_status
documentation: Parses the vote-status field.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_consensus_methods
documentation: Parses the consensus-methods field.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_consensus_method
documentation: Parses the consensus-method field.

Parses invalid published, valid-after, fresh-until, and valid-until fields.
All are simply datetime values.

documentation: Checks our time against both a valid and expired descriptor.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_is_fresh
documentation: Checks our time against both a fresh and unfresh descriptor.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_voting_delay
documentation: Parses the voting-delay field.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_version_lists
documentation:
Parses client-versions and server-versions fields. Both are comma separated
lists of tor versions.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_packages
documentation:
Parse the package line. These can appear multiple times, and have any
number of digests.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_known_flags
documentation:
Parses some known-flag entries. Just exercising the field, there's not much
to test here.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_flag_thresholds
documentation: Parses the flag-thresholds entry.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_parameters
documentation: Parses the parameters attributes.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_params
documentation: General testing for the 'params' line, exercising the happy cases.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_params_malformed
documentation: Parses a 'params' line with malformed content.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_params_range
documentation:
Check both the furthest valid 'params' values and values that are out of
bounds.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_params_misordered
documentation: Check that the 'params' line is rejected if out of order.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_footer_consensus_method_requirement
documentation:
Check that validation will notice if a footer appears before it was
introduced.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_footer_with_value
documentation: Tries to parse a descriptor with content on the 'directory-footer' line.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_wights_ok
documentation:
Parses a properly formed 'bandwidth-wights' line. Negative bandwidth
weights might or might not be valid. The spec doesn't say, so making sure
that we accept them.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_wights_malformed
documentation: Provides malformed content in the 'bandwidth-wights' line.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_wights_misordered
documentation: Check that the 'bandwidth-wights' line is rejected if out of order.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_wights_in_vote
documentation: Tries adding a 'bandwidth-wights' line to a vote.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_microdescriptor_signature
documentation:
The 'directory-signature' lines both with and without a defined method for
the signature format.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_malformed_signature
documentation: Provides malformed or missing content in the 'directory-signature' line.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_with_router_status_entries
documentation:
Includes router status entries within the document. This isn't to test the
RouterStatusEntry parsing but rather the inclusion of it within the
document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_with_microdescriptor_router_status_entries
documentation:
Includes microdescriptor flavored router status entries within the
document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_with_directory_authorities
documentation: Includes a couple directory authorities in the document.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_shared_randomness
documentation: Parses the shared randomness attributes.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_shared_randomness_malformed
documentation: Checks shared randomness with malformed values.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_file_headers
documentation: Parses a 'bandwidth-file-headers' line of votes.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_bandwidth_file_digest
documentation: Parses a 'bandwidth-file-digest' line of votes.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_with_legacy_directory_authorities
documentation: Includes both normal authorities and those following the '-legacy' format.

methods.name: test.unit.descriptor.networkstatus.document_v3.TestNetworkStatusDocument.test_authority_validation_flag_propagation
documentation:
Includes invalid certificate content in an authority entry. This is testing
that the 'validate' flag propagages from the document to authority, and
authority to certificate classes.

Simple container for listening to ControlSocket state changes and
rembembering them for the test.

Connects and closes the socket repeatedly. This is a simple attempt to
trigger concurrency issues.

Check that we can both receive asynchronous events while hammering our
socket with queries, and checks that when a controller is closed the

modules.name: test.integ.socket.control_socket
documentation:
Integration tests for the stem.socket.ControlSocket subclasses. When ran these
test basic functionality for a ControlPort *or* ControlSocketFile, depending on
which can connect to our tor instance.

These tests share a similar domain with those for the ControlMessage, but where
those focus on parsing and correctness of the content these are more concerned
with the behavior of the socket itself.

documentation: Checks that our connection_time method tracks when our state's changed.

documentation: Sends multiple requests before receiving back any of the replies.

documentation: Sends a message after we've closed the connection.

methods.name: test.integ.socket.control_socket.TestControlSocket.test_send_disconnected
documentation:
Sends a message to a socket that has been disconnected by the other end.

Our behavior upon disconnection slightly differs based on if we're a port
or socket file based connection. With a control port we won't notice the
disconnect (is_alive() will return True) until we've made a failed recv()
call. With a file socket, however, we'll also fail when calling send().

documentation: Receives a message after we've closed the connection.

methods.name: test.integ.socket.control_socket.TestControlSocket.test_recv_disconnected
documentation:
Receives a message from a socket that has been disconnected by the other
end.

methods.name: test.integ.socket.control_socket.TestControlSocket.test_connect_repeatedly
documentation: Checks that we can reconnect, use, and disconnect a socket repeatedly.

modules.name: test.require
documentation:
Testing requirements. This provides annotations to skip tests that shouldn't be
run.

```
Test Requirements
|- only_run_once - skip test if it has been ran before
|- needs - skips the test unless a requirement is met
|
|- cryptography - skips test unless the cryptography module is present
|- ed25519_support - skips test unless cryptography has ed25519 support
|- command - requires a command to be on the path
|- proc - requires the platform to have recognized /proc contents
|
|- controller - skips test unless tor provides a controller endpoint
|- version - skips test unless we meet a tor version requirement
|- ptrace - requires 'DisableDebuggerAttachment' to be set
+- online - skips unless targets allow for online tests
```
Copyright 2012-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.require.only_run_once
documentation:
Skips the test if it has ran before. If it hasn't then flags it as being ran.
This is useful to prevent lengthy tests that are independent of integ targets
from being run repeatedly with `RUN_ALL`.

functions.name: test.require.needs
documentation: Skips the test unless the conditional evaluates to 'true'.

functions.name: test.require.command
documentation: Skips the test unless a command is available on the path.

functions.name: test.require.version
documentation:
Skips the test unless we meet the required version.

:param stem.version.Version req_version: required tor version for the test

functions.name: test.require.version_older_than
documentation:
Skips the test unless we meet a version older than the requested version.
:param stem.version.Version req_version: the version that tor should be older than

modules.name: test.task
documentation:
{class}`~test.task.Task` that can be ran with {func}`~test.task.run_tasks` to initialize our tests. tasks are...

```
Initialization Tasks
|- STEM_VERSION - checks our stem version
|- STEM_COMMIT - checks our stem commit
|- TOR_VERSION - checks our tor version
|- TOR_COMMIT - checks our tor commit
|- PYTHON_VERSION - checks our python version
|- PLATFORM_VERSION - checks our operating system version
|- CRYPTO_VERSION - checks our version of cryptography
|- MOCK_VERSION - checks our version of mock
|- PYFLAKES_VERSION - checks our version of pyflakes
|- PYCODESTYLE_VERSION - checks our version of pycodestyle
|- CLEAN_PYC - removes any *.pyc without a corresponding *.py
|- REMOVE_TOR_DATA_DIR - removes our tor data directory
|- IMPORT_TESTS - ensure all test modules have been imported
|- UNUSED_TESTS - checks to see if any tests are missing from our settings
|- PYFLAKES_TASK - static checks
+- PYCODESTYLE_TASK - style checks
```
Copyright 2012-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.task._clean_orphaned_pyc
documentation:
Deletes any file with a *.pyc extention without a corresponding *.py.

:param list paths: paths to search for orphaned pyc files

functions.name: test.task._remove_tor_data_dir
documentation: Empties tor's data directory.

functions.name: test.task._import_tests
documentation:
Ensure all tests have been imported. This is important so tests can
register if they're asynchronous.

functions.name: test.task._check_for_unused_tests
documentation:
The 'test.unit_tests' and 'test.integ_tests' in our settings.cfg defines the
tests that we run. We do it this way so that we can control the order in
which our tests are run but there's a disadvantage: when we add new test
modules we can easily forget to add it there.

Checking to see if we have any unittest.TestCase subclasses not covered by
our settings.

:param list paths: paths to search for unused tests

functions.name: test.task.run
documentation:
Runs a series of {class}`test.Task` instances. This simply prints 'done'
or 'failed' for each unless we fail one that is marked as being required. If
that happens then we print its error message and call sys.exit().

```
param str category

label for the series of tasks

param list tasks

Task instances to be ran
```

classes.name: test.task.Task
documentation:
Task we can process while running our tests. The runner can return either a

modules.name: test.runner
documentation:
Runtime context for the integration tests. This is used both by the test runner
to start and stop tor, and by the integration tests themselves for information
about the tor test instance they're running against.

```
RunnerStopped - Runner doesn't have an active tor instance
TorInaccessable - Tor can't be queried for the information

exercise_controller - basic sanity check that a controller connection can be used
get_runner - Singleton for fetching our runtime context.

Runner - Runtime context for our integration tests.
  |- start - prepares and starts a tor instance for our tests to run against
  |- stop - stops our tor instance and cleans up any temporary files
  |- is_running - checks if our tor test instance is running
  |- is_accessible - checks if our tor instance can be connected to
  |- get_options - custom torrc options used for our test instance
  |- get_test_dir - testing directory path
  |- get_torrc_path - path to our tor instance's torrc
  |- get_torrc_contents - contents of our tor instance's torrc
  |- get_auth_cookie_path - path for our authentication cookie if we have one
  |- get_tor_cwd - current working directory of our tor process
  |- get_chroot - provides the path of our emulated chroot if we have one
  |- get_pid - process id of our tor process
  |- get_tor_socket - provides a socket to our test instance
  |- get_tor_controller - provides a controller for our test instance
  +- get_tor_command - provides the command used to start tor
```
Copyright 2011-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.runner.exercise_controller
documentation:
Checks that we can now use the socket by issuing a 'GETINFO config-file'
query. Controller can be either a {class}`stem.socket.ControlSocket` or
{class}`stem.control.BaseController`.

```

test being ran

param controller

```

modules.name: test.network
documentation:
Helper functions and classes to support tests which need to connect through
the tor network.

```
ProxyError - Base error for proxy issues.
  +- SocksError - Reports problems returned by the SOCKS proxy.

Socks - Communicate through a SOCKS5 proxy with a socket interface
```
Copyright 2012-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: test.network.negotiate_socks
documentation:
Negotiate with a socks4a server. Closes the socket and raises an exception on
failure.

```

socket connected to socks4a server

hostname/IP to connect to

param int port

port to connect to

raises

 if the socks server doesn't grant our request

returns

a list with the IP address and the port that the proxy connected to
```

classes.name: test.network.SocksError
documentation:

:var int code: error code returned by the SOCKS proxy

classes.name: test.network.Socks

methods.name: test.network.Socks.__init__
documentation:
Creates a SOCKS5-aware socket which will route connections through the
proxy_addr SOCKS5 proxy. Currently, only IPv4 TCP connections are
supported, so the defaults for family and [type] are your best option.

```
param tuple proxy_addr

address of the SOCKS5 proxy, for IPv4 this
contains (host, port)

param int family

address family of the socket

param int type

address type of the socket (see socket.socket for
more information about family and type)

returns

```

methods.name: test.network.Socks.connect_ex
documentation: Not Implemented.

A **socket.socket**-like interface through a SOCKS5 proxy connection.
Tor does not support proxy authentication, so neither does this class.

This class supports the context manager protocol. When used this way, the
socket will automatically close when leaving the context. An example:

```
from test.network import Socks

with Socks(('127.0.0.1', 9050)) as socks:
  socks.settimeout(2)
  socks.connect(('www.torproject.org', 443))
```

modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
Copyright 2012-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information
Release Checklist
* Recache latest information (cache_manual.py and cache_fallback_directories.py)
* Test with python2.6, python2.7, python3, and pypy.
|- If using tox run...
|    % tox -- --all --target RUN_ALL,ONLINE
|  Otherwise, for each interpreter run...
|    % [python_interpreter] run_tests.py --all --target RUN_ALL,ONLINE
|- Pypy test instructions for ubuntu are...
|    % sudo apt-get install pypy
|    % wget https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py
|    % pypy get-pip.py --user
|    % ~/.local/bin/pip install mock pycodestyle pyflakes --user
|    % pypy ./run_tests.py --all
+- Some version of python 3.x should be available in your platform's
repositories. To test against a specific version on ubuntu try the
following. In this example, Python 3.7...
% sudo apt-get install build-essential python-dev python-setuptools python-pip python-smbus
% sudo apt-get install libncursesw5-dev libgdbm-dev libc6-dev
% sudo apt-get install zlib1g-dev libsqlite3-dev tk-dev
% sudo apt-get install libssl-dev openssl libffi-dev
% wget https://www.python.org/ftp/python/3.7.0/Python-3.7.0.tgz
% tar -xzf Python-3.7.0.tgz
% mv Python-3.7.0 ~
% cd ~/Python-3.7.0
% ./configure
% make
% cd /path/to/stem
% ~/Python-3.7.0/python ./run_tests.py --all
* Tag the release
|- Bump stem's version (in stem/__init__.py and docs/index.rst).
|- git commit -a -m "Stem release 1.0.0"
|- git tag -u 9ABBEEC6 -m "stem release 1.0.0" 1.0.0 d0bb81a
+- git push --tags
* Dry-run release on https://pypi.org/project/stem/
|- python setup.py sdist --dryrun
|- gpg --detach-sig --armor dist/stem-dry-run-1.0.0.tar.gz
|- twine upload dist/*
+- Check that https://pypi.org/project/stem-dry-run/ looks correct, comparing it to https://pypi.org/project/stem/
+- Don't worry about the 'Bug Tracker' being missing. That's an attribute of the project itself.
* Final release
|- rm dist/*
|- python setup.py sdist
|- gpg --detach-sig --armor dist/stem-1.0.0.tar.gz
+- twine upload dist/*
* Contact package maintainers
* Announce the release (example: https://blog.torproject.org/blog/stem-release-11)

modules.name: stem.process
documentation:
Helper functions for working with tor as a process.

```
NO_TORRC

when provided as a torrc_path tor is ran with a blank configuration

DEFAULT_INIT_TIMEOUT

number of seconds before we time out our attempt to start a tor instance
```

**Module Overview:**

```
launch_tor             - starts up a tor process
launch_tor_with_config - starts a tor process with a custom torrc
```
Copyright 2011-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: stem.process.launch_tor
documentation:
Initializes a tor process. This blocks until initialization completes or we
error out.

If tor's data directory is missing or stale then bootstrapping will include
making several requests to the directory authorities which can take a little
while. Usually this is done in 50 seconds or so, but occasionally calls seem
to get stuck, taking well over the default timeout.

**To work to must log at NOTICE runlevel to stdout.** It does this by
default, but if you have a 'Log' entry in your torrc then you'll also need
'Log NOTICE stdout'.

Note: The timeout argument does not work on Windows or when outside the
main thread, and relies on the global state of the signal module.

:::{versionchanged} 1.6.0 Allowing the timeout argument to be a float.
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.7.0 Added the **close_output** argument.
:::

```
param str tor_cmd

command for starting tor

param list args

additional arguments for tor

param str torrc_path

location of the torrc for us to use

param int completion_percent

percent of bootstrap completion at which
this'll return

param functor init_msg_handler

optional functor that will be provided with
tor's initialization stdout as we get it

param int timeout

time after which the attempt to start tor is aborted, no
timeouts are applied if None

param bool take_ownership

asserts ownership over the tor process so it
aborts if this python process terminates or a 
we establish to it disconnects

param bool close_output

closes tor's stdout and stderr streams when
bootstrapping is complete if true

param str stdin

content to provide on stdin

returns

subprocess.Popen instance for the tor subprocess

raises

OSError if we either fail to create the tor process or reached a
timeout without success
```

functions.name: stem.process.launch_tor_with_config
documentation:
Initializes a tor process, like {func}`~stem.process.launch_tor`, but with a
customized configuration. This writes a temporary torrc to disk, launches
tor, then deletes the torrc.

For example...

```
tor_process = stem.process.launch_tor_with_config(
  config = {
    'ControlPort': '2778',
    'Log': [
      'NOTICE stdout',
      'ERR file /tmp/tor_error_log',
    ],
  },
)
```

:::{versionchanged} 1.7.0 Added the **close_output** argument.
:::

```
param dict config

configuration options, such as "{'ControlPort': '9051'}",
values can either be a str or list of str if for multiple values

param str tor_cmd

command for starting tor

param int completion_percent

percent of bootstrap completion at which
this'll return

param functor init_msg_handler

optional functor that will be provided with
tor's initialization stdout as we get it

param int timeout

time after which the attempt to start tor is aborted, no
timeouts are applied if None

param bool take_ownership

asserts ownership over the tor process so it
aborts if this python process terminates or a 
we establish to it disconnects

param bool close_output

closes tor's stdout and stderr streams when
bootstrapping is complete if true

returns

subprocess.Popen instance for the tor subprocess

raises

OSError if we either fail to create the tor process or reached a
timeout without success
```

modules.name: stem.response
documentation:
Parses replies from the control socket.

**Module Overview:**

```
convert - translates a ControlMessage into a particular response subclass

ControlMessage - Message that's read from the control socket.
  |- SingleLineResponse - Simple tor response only including a single line of information.
  |
  |- from_str - provides a ControlMessage for the given string
  |- is_ok - response had a 250 status
  |- content - provides the parsed message content
  +- raw_content - unparsed socket data

ControlLine - String subclass with methods for parsing controller responses.
  |- remainder - provides the unparsed content
  |- is_empty - checks if the remaining content is empty
  |- is_next_quoted - checks if the next entry is a quoted value
  |- is_next_mapping - checks if the next entry is a KEY=VALUE mapping
  |- peek_key - provides the key of the next entry
  |- pop - removes and returns the next entry
  +- pop_mapping - removes and returns the next entry as a KEY=VALUE mapping
```
Copyright 2012-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: stem.response.convert
documentation:
Converts a {class}`~stem.response.ControlMessage` into a particular kind of
tor response. This does an in-place conversion of the message from being a
{class}`~stem.response.ControlMessage` to a subclass for its response type.
Recognized types include...

| response_type     | Class                                                      |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| **ADD_ONION**     | {class}`stem.response.add_onion.AddOnionResponse`          |
| **AUTHCHALLENGE** | {class}`stem.response.authchallenge.AuthChallengeResponse` |
| **EVENT**         | {class}`stem.response.events.Event` subclass               |
| **GETCONF**       | {class}`stem.response.getconf.GetConfResponse`             |
| **GETINFO**       | {class}`stem.response.getinfo.GetInfoResponse`             |
| **MAPADDRESS**    | {class}`stem.response.mapaddress.MapAddressResponse`       |
| **PROTOCOLINFO**  | {class}`stem.response.protocolinfo.ProtocolInfoResponse`   |
| **SINGLELINE**    | {class}`stem.response.SingleLineResponse`                  |

```
param str response_type

type of tor response to convert to

param stem.response.ControlMessage message

message to be converted

param kwargs

optional keyword arguments to be passed to the parser method

raises

 the message isn't a proper response of
that type

 the arguments given as input are
invalid, this is can only be raised if the response_type is: GETINFO,
GETCONF

 the arguments given as input are
invalid, this is can only be raised if the response_type is:
MAPADDRESS

 if the action the event represents failed,
this is can only be raised if the response_type is: MAPADDRESS

TypeError if argument isn't a 
or response_type isn't supported
```

functions.name: stem.response._parse_entry
documentation:
Parses the next entry from the given space separated content.

:param str line: content to be parsed
:param bool quoted: parses the next entry as a quoted value, removing the quotes
:param bool escaped: unescapes the string

:returns: **tuple** of the form (entry, remainder)

:raises:
  * **ValueError** if quoted is True without the next value being quoted
  * **IndexError** if there's nothing to parse from the line

functions.name: stem.response._get_quote_indices
documentation:
Provides the indices of the next two quotes in the given content.

:param str line: content to be parsed
:param bool escaped: unescapes the string

:returns: **tuple** of two ints, indices being -1 if a quote doesn't exist

methods.name: stem.response.ControlMessage.is_ok
documentation:
Checks if any of our lines have a 250 response.

:returns: **True** if any lines have a 250 response code, **False** otherwise

methods.name: stem.response.ControlMessage.content
documentation:
Provides the parsed message content. These are entries of the form...

```
(status_code, divider, content)
```

**status_code**

: Three character code for the type of response (defined in section 4 of
  the control-spec).

**divider**

: Single character to indicate if this is mid-reply, data, or an end to the
  message (defined in section 2.3 of the control-spec).

**content**

: The following content is the actual payload of the line.

For data entries the content is the full multi-line payload with newline
linebreaks and leading periods unescaped.

The **status_code** and **divider** are both strings (**bytes** in python
2.x and **unicode** in python 3.x). The **content** however is **bytes** if
**get_bytes** is **True**.

:::{versionchanged} 1.1.0 Added the get_bytes argument.
:::

```
param bool get_bytes

provides bytes for the content rather than a str

returns

list of (str, str, str) tuples for the components of this message
```

methods.name: stem.response.ControlMessage.raw_content
documentation:
Provides the unparsed content read from the control socket.

:::{versionchanged} 1.1.0 Added the get_bytes argument.
:::

```
param bool get_bytes

if True then this provides bytes rather than a str

returns

str of the socket data used to generate this message
```

methods.name: stem.response.ControlMessage.__str__
documentation:
Content of the message, stripped of status code and divider protocol
formatting.

methods.name: stem.response.ControlMessage.__iter__
documentation:
Provides {class}`~stem.response.ControlLine` instances for the content of
the message. This is stripped of status codes and dividers, for instance...

```
250+info/names=
desc/id/* -- Router descriptors by ID.
desc/name/* -- Router descriptors by nickname.
.
250 OK
```

Would provide two entries...

```
1st - "info/names=
       desc/id/* -- Router descriptors by ID.
       desc/name/* -- Router descriptors by nickname."
2nd - "OK"
```

documentation:
Checks if our next entry is a KEY=VALUE mapping or not.

:param str key: checks that the key matches this value, skipping the check if **None**
:param bool quoted: checks that the mapping is to a quoted value
:param bool escaped: unescapes the string

documentation:
Provides the key of the next entry, providing **None** if it isn't a

methods.name: stem.response.ControlLine.pop
documentation:
Parses the next space separated entry, removing it and the space from our
remaining content. Examples...

```
>>> line = ControlLine("\"We're all mad here.\" says the grinning cat.")
>>> print line.pop(True)
  "We're all mad here."
>>> print line.pop()
  "says"
>>> print line.remainder()
  "the grinning cat."

>>> line = ControlLine("\"this has a \\\" and \\\\ in it\" foo=bar more_data")
>>> print line.pop(True, True)
  "this has a \" and \\ in it"
```

```
param bool quoted

parses the next entry as a quoted value, removing the quotes

param bool escaped

unescapes the string

returns

str of the next space separated entry

raises

ValueError if quoted is True without the value being quoted

IndexError if we don't have any remaining content left to parse
```

methods.name: stem.response.ControlLine.pop_mapping
documentation:
Parses the next space separated entry as a KEY=VALUE mapping, removing it
and the space from our remaining content.

:::{versionchanged} 1.6.0 Added the get_bytes argument.
:::

```
param bool quoted

parses the value as being quoted, removing the quotes

param bool escaped

unescapes the string

param bool get_bytes

provides bytes for the value rather than a str

returns

tuple of the form (key, value)

raises

ValueError if this isn't a KEY=VALUE mapping or if quoted is
True without the value being quoted

raises

IndexError if there's nothing to parse from the line
```

String subclass that represents a line of controller output. This behaves as
a normal string with additional methods for parsing and popping entries from
a space delimited series of elements like a stack.

None of these additional methods effect ourselves as a string (which is still
immutable). All methods are thread safe.

modules.name: stem.client
documentation:
Interaction with a Tor relay's ORPort. {class}`~stem.client.Relay` is
a wrapper for {class}`~stem.socket.RelaySocket`, much the same way as
{class}`~stem.control.Controller` provides higher level functions for
{class}`~stem.socket.ControlSocket`.

:::{versionadded} 1.7.0
:::

```
Relay - Connection with a tor relay's ORPort.
  | +- connect - Establishes a connection with a relay.
  |
  |- is_alive - reports if our connection is open or closed
  |- connection_time - time when we last connected or disconnected
  |- close - shuts down our connection
  |
  +- create_circuit - establishes a new circuit

Circuit - Circuit we've established through a relay.
  |- send - sends a message through this circuit
  +- close - closes this circuit
```
Copyright 2018-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

acceptable link protocol versions

raises

 if we're unable to establish a connection
```

provides bytes rather than parsing as a cell if True

next 
```

methods.name: stem.client.Relay._msg
documentation:
Sends a cell on the ORPort and provides the response we receive in reply.

Unfortunately unlike control sockets, ORPorts don't have generalized rules
for predictable message IO. With control sockets...

  * Each message we send receives a single reply.
  * We may also receive asynchronous events marked with a 650 status.

ORPorts by contrast receive variable length cells with differing rules on
their arrival. As such making a best effort attempt at a send-and-receive
method in which we do the following...

  * Discard any existing unread data from the socket.
  * Send our request.
  * Await up to a second for a reply.

It's quite possible this is a stupid approach. If so, patches welcome.

:param stem.client.cell.Cell cell: cell to be sent

:returns: **generator** with the cells received in reply

modules.name: stem.directory
documentation:
Directories that provide [relay descriptor information](../tutorials/mirror_mirror_on_the_wall.html). At a very high level tor works
as follows...

1. Volunteer starts a new tor relay, during which it sends a [server
   descriptor](descriptor/server_descriptor.html) to each of the directory
   authorities.
2. Each hour the directory authorities make a [vote](descriptor/networkstatus.html) that says who they think the active
   relays are in the network and some attributes about them.
3. The directory authorities send each other their votes, and compile that
   into the [consensus](descriptor/networkstatus.html). This document is very
   similar to the votes, the only difference being that the majority of the
   authorities agree upon and sign this document. The idividual relay entries
   in the vote or consensus is called [router status entries](descriptor/router_status_entry.html).
4. Tor clients (people using the service) download the consensus from an
   authority, fallback, or other mirror to determine who the active relays in
   the network are. They then use this to construct circuits and use the
   network.

```
Directory - Relay we can retrieve descriptor information from
  | |- from_cache - Provides cached information bundled with Stem.
  | +- from_remote - Downloads the latest directory information from tor.
  |
  |- Authority - Tor directory authority
  +- Fallback - Mirrors that can be used instead of the authorities
```

:::{versionadded} 1.7.0
:::
Copyright 2018-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information

functions.name: stem.directory._match_with
documentation:
Scans the given content against a series of regex matchers, providing back a
mapping of regexes to their capture groups. This maping is with the value if
the regex has just a single capture group, and a tuple otherwise.

:param list lines: text to parse
:param list regexes: regexes to match against
:param list required: matches that must be in the content

:returns: **dict** mapping matchers against their capture groups

:raises: **ValueError** if a required match is not present

methods.name: stem.directory.Directory.from_remote
documentation:
Reads and parses tor's directory data [from gitweb.torproject.org](https://gitweb.torproject.org/).
Note that while convenient, this reliance on GitWeb means you should alway
call with a fallback, such as...

```
try:
  authorities = stem.directory.Authority.from_remote()
except IOError:
  authorities = stem.directory.Authority.from_cache()
```

:::{versionadded} 1.5.0
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.7.0 Support added to the :class:`~stem.directory.Authority` class.
:::

```
param int timeout

seconds to wait before timing out the request

returns

dict of str identifiers to their


raises

IOError if unable to retrieve the fallback directories
```

Relay we can contact for descriptor information.

Our {func}`~stem.directory.Directory.from_cache` and
{func}`~stem.directory.Directory.from_remote` functions key off a
different identifier based on our subclass...

> - {class}`~stem.directory.Authority` keys off the nickname.
> - {class}`~stem.directory.Fallback` keys off fingerprints.

This is because authorities are highly static and canonically known by their
names, whereas fallbacks vary more and don't necessarily have a nickname to
key off of.

```
var str address

IPv4 address of the directory

var int or_port

port on which the relay services relay traffic

var int dir_port

port on which directory information is available

var str fingerprint

relay fingerprint

var str nickname

relay nickname

var str orport_v6

(address, port) tuple for the directory's IPv6
ORPort, or None if it doesn't have one
```

documentation:

methods.name: stem.directory.Fallback._write
documentation:
Persists fallback directories to a location in a way that can be read by
from_cache().

:param dict fallbacks: mapping of fingerprints to their fallback directory
:param str tor_commit: tor commit the fallbacks came from
:param str stem_commit: stem commit the fallbacks came from
:param dict headers: metadata about the file these came from
:param str path: location fallbacks will be persisted to

Particularly stable relays tor can instead of authorities when
bootstrapping. These relays are [hardcoded in tor](https://gitweb.torproject.org/tor.git/tree/src/or/fallback_dirs.inc).

For example, the following checks the performance of tor's fallback directories...

```
import time
from stem.descriptor.remote import get_consensus
from stem.directory import Fallback

for fallback in Fallback.from_cache().values():
  start = time.time()
  get_consensus(endpoints = [(fallback.address, fallback.dir_port)]).run()
  print('Downloading the consensus took %0.2f from %s' % (time.time() - start, fallback.fingerprint))
```

```
% python example.py
Downloading the consensus took 5.07 from 0AD3FA884D18F89EEA2D89C019379E0E7FD94417
Downloading the consensus took 3.59 from C871C91489886D5E2E94C13EA1A5FDC4B6DC5204
Downloading the consensus took 4.16 from 74A910646BCEEFBCD2E874FC1DC997430F968145
...
```

:::{versionadded} 1.5.0
:::

:::{versionchanged} 1.7.0 Added the has_extrainfo and header attributes which are part of the `second version of the fallback directories <https://lists.torproject.org/pipermail/tor-dev/2017-December/012721.html>`_.
:::

```
var bool has_extrainfo

True if the relay should be able to provide
extrainfo descriptors, False otherwise.

var collections.OrderedDict header

metadata about the fallback directory file this originated from
```

modules.name: stem.client.datatype
documentation:
Support for [Tor's ORPort protocol](https://gitweb.torproject.org/torspec.git/tree/tor-spec.txt).

**This module only consists of low level components, and is not intended for
users.** See our {class}`~stem.client.Relay` the API you probably want.

:::{versionadded} 1.7.0
:::

```
split - splits bytes into substrings

LinkProtocol - ORPort protocol version.

Field - Packable and unpackable datatype.
  |- LinkSpecifier - Communication method relays in a circuit.
  |    |- LinkByIPv4 - TLS connection to an IPv4 address.
  |    |- LinkByIPv6 - TLS connection to an IPv6 address.
  |    |- LinkByFingerprint - SHA1 identity fingerprint.
  |    +- LinkByEd25519 - Ed25519 identity fingerprint.
  |
  |- Size - Field of a static size.
  |- Address - Relay address.
  |- Certificate - Relay certificate.
  |
  |- pack - encodes content
  |- unpack - decodes content
  +- pop - decodes content with remainder

KDF - KDF-TOR derivatived attributes
  +- from_value - parses key material
```

```
.. data:: AddrType (enum)

  Form an address takes.

  ===================== ===========
  AddressType           Description
  ===================== ===========
  **HOSTNAME**          relay hostname
  **IPv4**              IPv4 address
  **IPv6**              IPv6 address
  **ERROR_TRANSIENT**   temporarily error retrieving address
  **ERROR_PERMANENT**   permanent error retrieving address
  **UNKNOWN**           unrecognized address type
  ===================== ===========
```

```
.. data:: RelayCommand (enum)

  Command concerning streams and circuits we've established with a relay.
  Commands have two characteristics...

  * **forward/backward**: **forward** commands are issued from the orgin,
    whereas **backward** come from the relay

  * **stream/circuit**: **steam** commands concern an individual steam, whereas
    **circuit** concern the entire circuit we've established with a relay

  ===================== ===========
  RelayCommand          Description
  ===================== ===========
  **BEGIN**             begin a stream (**forward**, **stream**)
  **DATA**              transmit data (**forward/backward**, **stream**)
  **END**               end a stream (**forward/backward**, **stream**)
  **CONNECTED**         BEGIN reply (**backward**, **stream**)
  **SENDME**            ready to accept more cells (**forward/backward**, **stream/circuit**)
  **EXTEND**            extend the circuit through another relay (**forward**, **circuit**)
  **EXTENDED**          EXTEND reply (**backward**, **circuit**)
  **TRUNCATE**          remove last circuit hop (**forward**, **circuit**)
  **TRUNCATED**         TRUNCATE reply (**backward**, **circuit**)
  **DROP**              ignorable no-op (**forward/backward**, **circuit**)
  **RESOLVE**           request DNS resolution (**forward**, **stream**)
  **RESOLVED**          RESOLVE reply (**backward**, **stream**)
  **BEGIN_DIR**         request descriptor (**forward**, **steam**)
  **EXTEND2**           ntor EXTEND request (**forward**, **circuit**)
  **EXTENDED2**         EXTEND2 reply (**backward**, **circuit**)
  **UNKNOWN**           unrecognized command
  ===================== ===========
```

```
.. data:: CertType (enum)

  Certificate purpose. For more information see...

    * `tor-spec.txt <https://gitweb.torproject.org/torspec.git/tree/tor-spec.txt>`_ section 4.2
    * `cert-spec.txt <https://gitweb.torproject.org/torspec.git/tree/cert-spec.txt>`_ section A.1
    * `rend-spec-v3.txt <https://gitweb.torproject.org/torspec.git/tree/rend-spec-v3.txt>`_ appendix E

  .. versionchanged:: 1.8.0
     Added the ED25519_SIGNING, LINK_CERT, ED25519_AUTHENTICATE,
     ED25519_IDENTITY, HS_V3_DESC_SIGNING, HS_V3_INTRO_AUTH, NTOR_ONION_KEY,
     and HS_V3_NTOR_ENC certificate types.

  ========================= ===========
  CertType                  Description
  ========================= ===========
  **LINK**                  link key certificate certified by RSA1024 identity
  **IDENTITY**              RSA1024 Identity certificate
  **AUTHENTICATE**          RSA1024 AUTHENTICATE cell link certificate
  **ED25519_SIGNING**       Ed25519 signing key, signed with identity key
  **LINK_CERT**             TLS link certificate, signed with ed25519 signing key
  **ED25519_AUTHENTICATE**  Ed25519 AUTHENTICATE cell key, signed with ed25519 signing key
  **ED25519_IDENTITY**      Ed25519 identity, signed with RSA identity
  **HS_V3_DESC_SIGNING**    hidden service v3 short-term descriptor signing key
  **HS_V3_INTRO_AUTH**      hidden service v3 introduction point authentication key
  **NTOR_ONION_KEY**        ntor onion key cross-certifying ed25519 identity key
  **HS_V3_NTOR_ENC**        hidden service v3 ntor-extra encryption key
  **UNKNOWN**               unrecognized certificate type
  ========================= ===========
```

```
.. data:: CloseReason (enum)

  Reason a relay is closed.

  ===================== ===========
  CloseReason           Description
  ===================== ===========
  **NONE**              no reason given
  **PROTOCOL**          tor protocol violation
  **INTERNAL**          internal error
  **REQUESTED**         client sent a TRUNCATE command
  **HIBERNATING**       relay suspended, trying to save bandwidth
  **RESOURCELIMIT**     out of memory, sockets, or circuit IDs
  **CONNECTFAILED**     unable to reach relay
  **OR_IDENTITY**       connected, but its OR identity was not as expected
  **OR_CONN_CLOSED**    connection that was carrying this circuit died
  **FINISHED**          circuit has expired for being dirty or old
  **TIMEOUT**           circuit construction took too long
  **DESTROYED**         circuit was destroyed without a client TRUNCATE
  **NOSUCHSERVICE**     request was for an unknown hidden service
  **UNKNOWN**           unrecognized reason
  ===================== ===========
```
Copyright 2018-2019, Damian Johnson and The Tor Project
See LICENSE for licensing information
